activities_projects.title: Area of a Triangle by Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of a triangle using Heron’s formula
steps: Take a triangle with sides a = 5 cm, b = 6 cm, c = 7 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2
Use Heron’s formula √[s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)] to find area
Write the area in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how to use Heron’s formula

activities_projects.title: Measuring Triangle Sides and Area
activities_projects.objective: Measure triangle sides and calculate area using Heron’s formula
steps: Draw a triangle on paper
Measure all three sides
Calculate semi-perimeter and area using Heron’s formula
Record results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula on measured triangles
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement and area calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Area with Base × Height Method
activities_projects.objective: Compare area calculated by Heron’s formula with base × height method
steps: Draw a triangle and measure base and height
Calculate area using ½ × base × height
Also calculate area using Heron’s formula
Compare both areas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Verifies Heron’s formula with traditional method

activities_projects.title: Area of Equilateral Triangle using Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of equilateral triangle using Heron’s formula
steps: Take an equilateral triangle with sides 6 cm each
Calculate semi-perimeter s = (6+6+6)/2
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
Record the area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula to equilateral triangles

activities_projects.title: Area of Isosceles Triangle using Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of isosceles triangle using Heron’s formula
steps: Take an isosceles triangle with sides a = b = 5 cm, c = 6 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2
Use Heron’s formula to find area
Record the area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula to isosceles triangles

activities_projects.title: Drawing Triangles of Given Area
activities_projects.objective: Draw triangles with sides having approximate area using Heron’s formula
steps: Choose three sides such that a + b > c
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
Draw the triangle on paper with measured sides
Verify the drawn triangle area approximately matches calculated area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Practically applies Heron’s formula in drawing
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct triangle drawing and approximate area verification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Triangles with Same Semi-perimeter
activities_projects.objective: Compare areas of different triangles with same semi-perimeter
steps: Take two triangles with sides giving same s
Calculate area of both using Heron’s formula
Observe which triangle has larger area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of side lengths on area

activities_projects.title: Area of Right-Angled Triangle using Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area using Heron’s formula and compare with ½ × base × height
steps: Take a right-angled triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm
Calculate area using ½ × base × height
Also calculate area using Heron’s formula
Compare results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Verifies Heron’s formula works for right-angled triangles

activities_projects.title: Practical Measurement of Triangle Area
activities_projects.objective: Measure triangle on ground or paper and calculate area using Heron’s formula
steps: Measure three sides of a triangular area on ground or paper
Calculate semi-perimeter
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
Record observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula to real-world measurements

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangle with Decimals
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of triangle with decimal side lengths
steps: Take triangle with sides a = 5.5 cm, b = 6.2 cm, c = 4.8 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to find area
Record the result
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula with decimal side lengths
